subject: Sociology
title: Sociology and Public Policy
description: A fast breakdown of Sociology and Public Policy for builders.

hook.text: A city wants to reduce crime. Should it hire more police or fund after-school programs? Sociological data answers this.

buildup.text: Sociology informs policy by revealing root causes. Poverty, not laziness, drives most petty crime.

discovery.text: The Moving to Opportunity study showed that moving families to better neighborhoods boosted children's earnings.

twist.text: Politicians often ignore sociological evidence when it contradicts popular beliefs or donor interests.

climax.text: Evidence-based policy works, but only when research reaches decision-makers and they choose to listen.

punchline.text: Good policy starts with understanding the problem, not guessing.

quiz.question: What did the Moving to Opportunity study show?
options: Moving to better neighborhoods improved children's earnings
Police presence is the best crime deterrent
Individual motivation matters most for success
